exit_gate.target: p95 click->ACCESS < 5000 ms for BOTH maya-thriving (employee -> /profile) AND dan-manager (manager -> /enterprise/...), over 5 consecutive cold reset-to-seed runs
exit_gate.environment: billion.taildc510.ts.net (Linux VM, 7.3 GiB RAM), demo-1, over the tailnet origin
exit_gate.warm_db_caveat: iter-04's 1456/1400 ms figures were measured on a WARM database (F-9: --purge had never purged on Linux, so every 'cold' bring-up for days reused the same DB and images). They are NOT the gate number. The gate number is iter-05's 2413/1767 ms, over 5 cycles each PROVEN cold (initdb re-ran) and PROVEN green before being measured.

tests.python_delta_vs_m217: +20 passing (demo-stack 462->495 incl. +12 close fences; stack-injection 194->186 counts PASSING only, M217's 194 was total)
tests.python_counting_method: JUnit XML (pytest --junit-xml), parsed. NEVER grepped from piped stdout.
tests.note: Go count uses `grep -c '^func Test'` across all 6 go.mod modules — the SAME method M217 recorded (1750), so the +34 is like-for-like.

deliberately_red_genes.why: F-11 / D16 — the fake BAPI fabricates organization.public_metadata.eid as 'org_eid_'+orgID instead of the roster's real org UUID (the ORG-level twin of the user stub that cost this milestone ~6 s/render). NOT fixed here: resources.go is the demo's RUNTIME path and the 5-cycle cold gate was graded on the current binary (iter-05 D13), so a post-gate runtime change would ship something other than what was measured. Landed RED so the score stops lying — a silently-omitted field is how the headline bug survived four releases.

unmeasured.why: dependency-gated: without @clerk/express node_modules the runner cannot build — exits rc=2 with NO score, and nothing treats that as a failure. Reproduced identically at baseline f296e5e => PRE-EXISTING, not a regression.
unmeasured.consequence: iter-04's 'all 5 surfaces 100%' claim is NOT reproducible on this box. Corrected in progress.md, iter-04/decisions.md, and 7 rext/corpus docs.

bugs_fixed.close_breakdown: 1 misfiled regression (stack-injection fence, RED since iter-03) + 3 must-fix false-greens (MF-1 purge collateral leak, MF-2 stale-image green, MF-3 skipped-patch green) + cmd_up verdict + ts field write-only + sync.Once/deleted-binary + latency-out committed + LATENCY_RUNS NaN + purge diagnostics to /dev/null + x/crypto bump

to_M220: C-5 vendor clerk-js + bound the unbounded Timeout:0
to_M221: F-7 NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_API_URL blackhole twin (the loaded gun)
C-3 cms/Directus 403s on the CONTENT path
carry_forward.note: All 9 are Fate-3 and the receiving milestones' overview.md files were EDITED (verified). None could land in M218 without invalidating its own exit gate: a demo-runtime change restarts the 5-cycle cold battery (iter-05 D13 / D16). Zero escape-hatch deferrals — nothing leaves v2.3.

deferral_audit.repeat_resolved: x/crypto@v0.52.0 — deferred at v2.2, re-fated Fate-1 to 'M218's rext roll', still not landed at close. AGED_OUT + REPEAT => LAND-NOW. Bumped 0.51.0 -> 0.52.0; alignment gate score IDENTICAL pre/post (97.2%/100%), which is how we know it is behaviour-neutral.

known_issues.what: dev-stack test suite fails on this box
known_issues.status: environmental — needs a live Postgres on :15432, and this box's .agentspace/secrets lacks critical keys so the secret-coverage pre-flight aborts before the N-guard. Identical at v2.2 and M217.
known_issues.m218_effect: none — M218 does not touch dev-stack/
known_issues.action: flagged in the Gate Outcome Ledger for user sign-off; NOT an M218 regression

notes: The defining finding of the close: the hardening ledger MISCLASSIFIED an M218 regression as 'pre-existing, M217 footprint' because it used f296e5e — M218's OWN iter-05 commit — as the 'baseline'. Re-measured against the true pre-M218 ref (cue-to-cue-m217), stack-injection::test_next_web_block_shape PASSES there and FAILS at HEAD: iter-03's own fix added WUNDERGRAPH_SSR_ENDPOINT and left the exact-shape fence pinned to the old block. 'Reproduced at baseline => pre-existing' is only sound if the baseline predates the milestone. That is D17 (the stale-verdict hazard) surfacing inside the pass convened to name it — the sixth instance in one milestone. Three further false-greens (MF-1/2/3) were the same class, and MF-3 sat directly on the gate path: a SKIPPED demo-patch wrote nothing to demopatch.log, so a stack running WITHOUT the headline SSR fix would have printed '✓ demo-patches: none refused', graded green, and been measured by run-latency.sh.
